1	{
2	  "phase": "question",
3	  "questions": [
4	    {
5	      "id": "q1",
6	      "question": "The backend already has SSE (Server-Sent Events) endpoints at /v1/markets/{id}/stream and /v1/accounts/{id}/risk/stream. Should we use these existing SSE endpoints (EventSource API) instead of adding WebSocket infrastructure, or does the task specifically require WebSocket protocol?",
7	      "category": "requirements",
8	      "why_it_matters": "SSE infrastructure is already built and tested on the backend. Adding WebSocket would require new backend code (ws library, upgrade handling, connection management) duplicating what SSE already provides. Using SSE means only frontend work is needed."
9	    },
10	    {
11	      "id": "q2",
12	      "question": "Which SSE event types should drive UI updates? The backend streams market_snapshot, terminal_event, and market_marginals for markets, plus risk_snapshot and risk_update for accounts. Should we subscribe to all of these or only market_marginals for price changes?",
13	      "category": "requirements",
14	      "why_it_matters": "Subscribing to all event types means more complex state management but richer real-time UX. Subscribing only to marginals is simpler but misses status changes and trade events."
15	    },
16	    {
17	      "id": "q3",
18	      "question": "Should the real-time connection fully replace the current React Query polling (refetchInterval: 5000ms), or should SSE updates supplement polling as an optimistic layer with polling as fallback?",
19	      "category": "design",
20	      "why_it_matters": "Replacing polling entirely is cleaner but requires robust reconnection logic. Keeping polling as fallback is more resilient but means maintaining two data paths."
21	    },
22	    {
23	      "id": "q4",
24	      "question": "Should price updates include visual feedback (flash/highlight animation) when a probability changes in real-time, or just silently update the displayed values?",
25	      "category": "requirements",
26	      "why_it_matters": "Visual feedback makes real-time updates obvious to users but adds UI complexity. Silent updates are simpler but users may not notice changes."
27	    },
28	    {
29	      "id": "q5",
30	      "question": "Which views need real-time updates? MarketDetail page only, or also MarketList (all markets overview), Analytics/PriceChart, and Portfolio views?",
31	      "category": "requirements",
32	      "why_it_matters": "MarketDetail is the most impactful and simplest scope. Expanding to MarketList means potentially many concurrent SSE connections. Analytics/charts need different update logic (appending data points vs replacing state)."
33	    }
34	  ],
35	  "assumptions": [
36	    {
37	      "id": "a1",
38	      "assumption": "We should use the existing SSE backend endpoints rather than adding WebSocket protocol support, since SSE is already implemented and sufficient for server-to-client price push.",
39	      "confidence": 0.85,
40	      "if_wrong": "Would need to add a WebSocket library to the Python backend (e.g. websockets), implement upgrade handling, duplicate the subscriber/publisher logic, and use the browser WebSocket API instead of EventSource on the frontend."
41	    },
42	    {
43	      "id": "a2",
44	      "assumption": "The frontend should use a custom React hook wrapping EventSource that integrates with the existing React Query cache, invalidating/updating cached market data when SSE events arrive.",
45	      "confidence": 0.9,
46	      "if_wrong": "Would need a separate state management layer (e.g. Zustand or Redux) alongside React Query, increasing complexity."
47	    },
48	    {
49	      "id": "a3",
50	      "assumption": "The initial scope is MarketDetail page only - subscribing to /v1/markets/{id}/stream when viewing a specific market and updating marginals + events in real-time.",
51	      "confidence": 0.7,
52	      "if_wrong": "If MarketList also needs real-time updates, we'd need a connection manager to handle multiple concurrent SSE streams or a single aggregate endpoint."
53	    },
54	    {
55	      "id": "a4",
56	      "assumption": "SSE reconnection can be handled by the browser's native EventSource auto-reconnect behavior, with the fromSeq query parameter for message replay on reconnect.",
57	      "confidence": 0.8,
58	      "if_wrong": "Would need custom reconnection logic with exponential backoff and manual EventSource recreation."
59	    },
60	    {
61	      "id": "a5",
62	      "assumption": "Polling can be reduced (e.g. 30s fallback) rather than eliminated entirely, keeping React Query as the source of truth with SSE updates feeding into the cache.",
63	      "confidence": 0.75,
64	      "if_wrong": "If polling must be fully removed, the SSE hook becomes the sole data source and needs more robust error handling and initial data loading."
65	    }
66	  ],
67	  "decomposition_assessment": {
68	    "complexity_score": 2,
69	    "complexity_reason": "Backend SSE infrastructure already exists. Work is primarily frontend: one custom hook for EventSource, integration with React Query cache, and updating 1-2 components. Estimated ~3-4 files changed, ~150-250 lines of new code.",
70	    "uncertainty_score": 2,
71	    "uncertainty_reason": "The SSE backend is already built and documented. EventSource API is well-understood. Main uncertainty is whether task literally requires WebSocket vs SSE, and exact scope of which views get real-time updates.",
72	    "independent_subproblems": [
73	      "Create useMarketStream hook wrapping EventSource with React Query cache integration",
74	      "Wire hook into MarketDetail page and update probability display components",
75	      "Add visual feedback (optional flash animation) for price changes"
76	    ],
77	    "decision": "PROCEED_ALONE",
78	    "decision_reason": "Complexity 2 and uncertainty 2 are both low. The backend work is done, so this is a focused frontend task touching 3-4 files. All subproblems are closely coupled (hook -> integration -> UI) and share state, making parallel execution impractical."
79	  },
80	  "spawn_children": false,
81	  "children": []
82	}

- The actual event types and their payload structures

1	# T556 Scope And Compatibility Boundary Freeze
2	
3	Date: 2026-04-08
4	Branch: `ff/Ttemporal-fleet-as9-r1-analysis-scope-and-contract`
5	
6	## Question
7	
8	Freeze three decisions for T556:
9	
10	1. Resolve the wording gap between the task title shorthand ("LMSR cost function") and the epic wording (`Implement per-user asset model transformation (Sx = b ln q(x))`).
11	2. Confirm whether the existing HTTP, event, and risk envelopes stay stable while the asset math is migrated.
12	3. Lock the source of the LMSR liquidity parameter `b`.
13	
14	## Findings
15	
16	### 1. The epic already places T556 on the risk/solvency path, not on the public API surface
17	
18	The current planning docs consistently place T556 inside the internal solvency engine work:
19	
20	- `docs/t537-epic-execution-baseline.md` defines T556 as `Implement per-user asset model transformation (Sx = b ln q(x))`.
21	- `docs/t538-scope-matrix.md` groups T556 with T557, T558, T559, and T561 under `Risk/solvency`.
22	- `docs/t539-mvp-launch-gate.md` puts T556 under the solvency gate whose acceptance criterion is that no accepted command can make user min-assets negative.
23	
24	That grouping matters. It means T556 is not the task that owns public request/response redesign, event-version changes, or a new market schema. It owns the internal asset-model replacement that later solvency checks depend on.
25	
26	### 2. "LMSR cost function" and "per-user asset model transformation" describe the same seam from different sides
27	
28	The title wording focuses on the mechanism: an LMSR-style cost/asset model with liquidity parameter `b`.
29	
30	The epic wording focuses on the state representation that mechanism produces: a per-user asset transformation `Sx = b ln q(x)`.
31	
32	Those are not competing scopes. They are two views of the same internal migration:
33	
34	- the engine side introduces LMSR-backed cost/asset math
35	- the state side uses that math to transform and maintain the user's asset state
36	
37	The safe interpretation is therefore:
38	
39	- T556 owns the internal math/state transition layer
40	- T557 owns the unconditional pre-acceptance guard
41	- T558 owns conditional admissible-range logic
42	- T559 owns the GS minimum-assets path
43	- T561 owns rejection-reason and guidance policy
44	
45	So T556 should supply the asset primitives those tasks consume, not absorb their public-contract work.
46	
47	### 3. The checked-in server already has stable public envelopes that the migration should preserve
48	
49	The current branch already exposes and tests a coherent compatibility boundary:
50	
51	- ProbabilityEdit HTTP requests use the thin market-scoped REST body, not a client-supplied `bayes-command/v1` envelope.
52	- Accepted ProbabilityEdit writes return `201` with an order/result response model.
53	- Unconditional solvency failures return `409` with `error/result/meta` and `min_asset_violation`.
54	- Terminal events are persisted and read back as `bayes-event/v1` envelopes with `CommandAccepted` or `CommandRejected`.
55	- `GET /v1/accounts/{id}/risk` exposes a stable read model with `minAssets` and `capacityIndicators`.
56	
57	Evidence in the live checkout:
58	
59	- `backend/server.py`
60	  - `build_terminal_acceptance_response(...)`
61	  - `build_terminal_rejection_response(...)`
62	  - `emit_terminal_event(...)`
63	  - `get_account_risk(...)`
64	- `tests/test_bayes_market_api.py`
65	  - market-scoped ProbabilityEdit HTTP coverage
66	  - journal-chain coverage
67	  - read-after-write risk coverage
68	  - unconditional solvency rejection coverage
69	
70	Verification run on this branch:
71	
72	```bash
73	python3 -m unittest discover -s tests
74	```
75	
76	Result:
77	
78	```text
79	Ran 135 tests in 4.382s
80	
81	OK
82	```
83	
84	So the migration boundary is already test-anchored. T556 should change the internal asset math behind these surfaces, not redefine the surfaces.
85	
86	### 4. The current placeholder solvency seam is intentionally temporary and points directly at T556/T559
87	
88	`docs/t557-unconditional-solvency-contract-freeze.md` is explicit that the current placeholder account-risk model is not the final asset engine and is only frozen until `T556`/`T559` replace the math.
89	
90	That document already freezes an important compatibility principle:
91	
92	- the pre-acceptance rejection boundary is stable
93	- the public rejection code and payload shape are stable
94	- the current implementation seam is temporary
95	
96	This is strong evidence that T556 is supposed to replace the underlying asset computation without reopening the surrounding HTTP/event/risk contracts.
97	
98	### 5. `market.liquidity` is the only existing market-level parameter that fits LMSR `b` without widening the contract
99	
100	In the checked-in backend:
101	
102	- every market record already carries numeric `liquidity`
103	- `GET /v1/markets` includes `liquidity` in the summary shape
104	- `GET /v1/markets/{id}` returns the full market object, which also includes `liquidity`
105	- there is no parallel `b`, `lmsrB`, `costFunction`, or `liquidityParameter` field anywhere in the current market schema
106	
107	At the same time, the current ProbabilityEdit placeholder math does not consume `market.liquidity`; it uses KL-divergence-based `impactScore` and account-level min-asset bookkeeping.
108	
109	That makes the migration choice straightforward:
110	
111	- if T556 needs a per-market LMSR depth parameter, the stable in-tree slot for it is `market.liquidity`
112	- adding a second parameter field would be a public schema expansion with no current need
113	- renaming `liquidity` to `b` would be a breaking read-contract change
114	
115	So the compatibility-safe decision is to freeze:
116	
117	```text
118	market.liquidity == LMSR b
119	```
120	
121	internally, while keeping the public field name and JSON placement unchanged.
122	
123	### 6. The external read models should remain summaries even if the internal asset state becomes richer
124	
125	The epic wording implies a richer per-user asset representation than the current placeholder scalar capacity model.
126	
127	But the current public read surfaces do not expose a full per-state asset vector. They expose summaries:
128	
129	- acceptance events publish `assetDelta` entries with `beforeMinAsset` and `afterMinAsset`
130	- rejection responses publish preview scalars such as `beforeMinAsset`, `impactScore`, and `afterMinAsset`
131	- the account risk endpoint publishes `minAssets.overall`, per-market `minAsset`, and `capacityIndicators`
132	
133	That summary boundary should remain intact during T556. A richer internal asset representation is compatible with the current public contracts as long as the server still projects it back to the same scalar min-asset summaries.
134	
135	## Freeze Decision
136	
137	Freeze T556 as an internal asset-engine migration with a strict compatibility boundary:
138	
139	### 1. Scope
140	
141	T556 is the task that replaces the placeholder asset/cost math with the LMSR-backed per-user asset transformation described in the epic.
142	
143	It should be read as:
144	
145	- implementing the internal asset representation and update rule
146	- providing the math used by later solvency checks and previews
147	- keeping the surrounding public API/event/read-model contracts stable
148	
149	It should not be read as:
150	
151	- a public HTTP contract rewrite
152	- an event-schema version bump
153	- a risk-endpoint redesign
154	- an EventTrade formula-contract rewrite
155	- a new market-schema expansion for a separate `b` field
156	
157	### 2. Compatibility Boundary
158	
159	During the T556 migration, keep these surfaces stable:
160	
161	- `POST /v1/markets/{id}/orders/probability-edit` request shape
162	- ProbabilityEdit acceptance status/result envelope
163	- ProbabilityEdit rejection status/code/result envelope
164	- `bayes-event/v1` terminal event envelope shape and event types
165	- `CommandRejected.reasonCode = "min_asset_violation"` for the existing unconditional solvency path
166	- `GET /v1/accounts/{id}/risk` response envelope and key names
167	- market list/detail field names, including `liquidity`
168	
169	Internal formulas, stored asset state, and how min-asset summaries are derived may change behind that boundary.
170	
171	### 3. LMSR Parameter Freeze
172	
173	Freeze `market.liquidity` as the LMSR liquidity parameter `b`.
174	
175	Implementation consequence:
176	
177	- T556 should read `market.liquidity` as the internal `b` input
178	- T556 should not introduce a second market parameter carrying the same meaning
179	- T556 should not rename the public field from `liquidity` to `b`
180	
181	## Consequence
182	
183	This resolves the wording gap as follows:
184	
185	- the task-title shorthand "LMSR cost function" names the internal mechanism
186	- the epic wording "per-user asset model transformation" names the state model produced by that mechanism
187	
188	For implementation planning, treat T556 as the internal math/state foundation for the solvency stack, with no public envelope changes required.
189	
190	That keeps T556 aligned with:
191	
192	- T557/T558/T559/T561 as follow-on solvency contracts
193	- the existing market-scoped HTTP boundary
194	- the frozen event/journal surfaces
195	- the current account-risk read model
196	- the already-published `market.liquidity` field as the single LMSR depth knob

**File: `/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/lib/query/hooks.ts` (lines 132-271)**

**File: `/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/lib/query/hooks.ts`**

- `refetchMarketRouteQueries()` (lines 62-65) - Full market data refresh

Invalidations triggered on mutations: create market, probability edits, resolutions, event trades, and comments.

**Portfolio Page** (`/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/routes/Portfolio.tsx`):
- Uses `useMarkets()`, `useAccountExposure()`, `useAccountRisk()`
- Displays live positions with real-time market data mapping

**System Status Page** (`/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/routes/System.tsx`):
- Uses `useHealth()`, `useServiceIndex()`, `useMarkets()`
- Displays API health beacon and platform statistics

**Discussion Thread** (`/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/features/market/DiscussionThread.tsx`):
- Uses `useMarketComments()`, `usePostMarketComment()`
- Reads and writes market comments with mutation callbacks

### Summary

Your frontend uses a **hybrid polling + WebSocket architecture**:
- **Polling**: Markets and market details poll every 5 seconds via React Query's `refetchInterval`
- **WebSocket**: Active markets have real-time price subscriptions for instant updates
- **Smart invalidation**: WebSocket updates trigger targeted query invalidations
- **Auto-reconnect**: WebSocket connection has exponential backoff (500ms to 5s)
- **React Query caching**: 2-second stale time with intelligent query key structure enables effective deduplication across pages

Excellent! I now have enough information to provide a comprehensive report. Let me compile all findings:

## Research Report: Prediction Market Frontend Codebase

### 1. **PROBABILITY/PRICE DISPLAY COMPONENTS**

**Primary Component: ProbabilityBar**
- **Location:** `/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/components/ui/ProbabilityBar.tsx` (lines 1-51)
- **Features:**
  - Displays outcomes as segmented horizontal bar with proportional widths based on marginal probabilities
  - Color-coded outcomes (8-color palette: green, red, blue, yellow, purple, pink, teal, orange)
  - Shows outcome names with percentage labels below the bar
  - Dynamic width calculation: `width: ${p * 100}%` where p is probability value
  - Smooth animation on probability changes: `transition: "width 0.3s ease"` (line 34)
  - Accepts `outcomes: MarketOutcome[]` and `marginals: Record<string, number>` props

**Secondary Components Using Probabilities:**
- **VariableRow** (line 98): Mini probability bar with inline editing, `transition: "width 0.2s ease"`
- **JunctionTreePanel** (line 33): Clique state capacity bar showing state distribution, `transition: "width 0.3s ease"`
- **EventTradePanel** (lines 24, 157): Displays price as percentage format `${(price * 100).toFixed(1)}%`
- **AssumptionPanel + AssumptionBar**: Shows conditional probabilities given assumptions

**Probability Formatting:**
- **Location:** `/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/lib/utils/format.ts` (lines 1-3)
- Function: `formatProbability(p: number): string` returns `${(p * 100).toFixed(1)}%`

---

### 2. **ANIMATION & TRANSITION EFFECTS**

**CSS Transitions (Inline Styles):**
1. **ProbabilityBar width transition** - `transition: "width 0.3s ease"` (line 34)
2. **MarketList card border hover** - `transition: "border-color 0.15s"` (line 79)
3. **VariableRow probability bar** - `transition: "width 0.2s ease"` (line 98)
4. **JunctionTreePanel clique bar** - `transition: "width 0.3s ease"` (line 33)

**Keyframe Animations:**
- **Spinner Component** - `/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/components/ui/Spinner.tsx` (lines 7-9)
  - Rotation animation: `@keyframes spin { to { transform: rotate(360deg) } }`
  - Applied to SVG with `animation: "spin 1s linear infinite"` (line 7)
  - Used for loading states throughout app

**Interactive Hover Effects:**
- MarketList cards (lines 81-82): Dynamic border color change on hover using `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave`
- EventTradePanel buttons: Opacity changes based on disabled state (line 189)
- Navigation links: Color changes based on active state (lines 48-53 in App.tsx)

**No External Animation Libraries:** No framer-motion, react-spring, or similar - purely CSS transitions and inline styles.

---

### 3. **COMPONENT HIERARCHY**

**Root Structure:**
```
AppLayout (App.tsx)
├── Header (NavLinks: Markets, Portfolio, System)
├── Main Content Area
│   └── Outlet (React Router)
│       ├── MarketList
│       │   ├── StatusBadge (per market)
│       │   └── Link cards (styled divs)
│       │
│       ├── MarketDetail (marketId)
│       │   ├── ProbabilityBar (main)
│       │   ├── PositionCard (if configured)
│       │   ├── ResolveMarketPanel
│       │   ├── AssumptionProvider
│       │   │   ├── AssumptionPanel
│       │   │   │   ├── AssumptionBar
│       │   │   │   └── VariableRow[] (per market)
│       │   │   │       └── Mini probability bar + edit controls
│       │   ├── EventTradePanel (trading UI)
│       │   ├── DiscussionThread
│       │   ├── BayesNetGraph (SVG visualization)
│       │   ├── Event Journal (table with EventRow items)
│       │   └── JunctionTreePanel
│       │       ├── Engine info banner
│       │       ├── Junction tree summary
│       │       ├── CliqueCard[] (bars)
│       │       └── InferenceDiagnostics (metrics grid)
│       │
│       ├── Portfolio
│       │   ├── MetricCard[] (capacity indicators)
│       │   └── Live Outcome Holdings (table)
│       │
│       └── System
│           ├── Health beacon (animated dot)
│           ├── Platform Stats (CountCard[])
│           ├── Markets status breakdown
│           └── API Surface routes
└── ErrorBoundary wrapper
```

---

### 4. **STYLING APPROACH**

**CSS Framework: CSS Variables + Inline Styles (NO Tailwind/CSS Modules)**

**Global CSS Variables** - `/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/styles/globals.css` (lines 1-52):
- **Colors:** `--color-bg`, `--color-bg-surface`, `--color-border`, `--color-text`, `--color-primary`, `--color-success`, `--color-danger`, `--color-warning`, `--color-info`, `--color-resolved`, `--color-closed`, `--color-draft`, `--color-active`
- **Sizing:** `--radius-sm` (4px), `--radius-md` (8px), `--radius-lg` (12px)
- **Spacing:** `--space-xs` (4px), `--space-sm` (8px), `--space-md` (16px), `--space-lg` (24px), `--space-xl` (32px)
- **Typography:** `--font-mono`, `--font-sans`

**Styling Pattern:**
All components use inline `style` objects with CSS variables. Examples:
- ProbabilityBar: `style={{ display: "flex", height: 24, borderRadius: "var(--radius-sm)" }}`
- EventTradePanel: Styled objects like `panelStyle`, `sideBtnStyle`, `selectStyle` (lines 221-290)
- VariableRow: `rowStyle`, `assumedRowStyle`, `targetRowStyle` (lines 176-230)

**Dark Theme Colors (from globals.css):**
- Background: `#0f1117` (very dark blue-gray)
- Surface: `#1a1d27` (dark blue-gray)
- Text: `#e4e5ea` (light gray)
- Primary: `#6366f1` (indigo)

---

### 5. **VIEWS/PAGES**

**Route Definitions** - `/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/app/router.tsx` (lines 1-21):

| Route | Component | Purpose |
|-------|-----------|---------|
| `/` | Redirect to `/markets` | Default redirect |
| `/markets` | MarketList | Browse all markets with filtering by status |
| `/markets/new` | CreateMarketForm | Create new prediction market |
| `/markets/:marketId` | MarketDetail | Market detail with probabilities, trading, graphs, analytics |
| `/portfolio` | Portfolio | User's trading positions and account risk metrics |
| `/system` | System | System health, platform-wide stats, API surface |

**Key View Features:**

**MarketList View:**
- Grid layout: `gridTemplateColumns: "repeat(auto-fill, minmax(360px, 1fr))"` (line 66)
- Status filter dropdown (5 options: all, active, resolved, closed, draft)
- Displays volume, liquidity, time until expiry per market
- Card hover effect with border color transition

**MarketDetail View:**
- Full market information with description
- ProbabilityBar component for outcomes
- Position card showing account risk metrics
- Resolve market panel (if applicable)
- Assumption panel for conditional probability editing (line 58)
- Quick trade panel for EventTrade orders
- Discussion thread
- Bayes net graph visualization
- Event journal (scrollable table of market events)
- Junction tree panel with engine diagnostics

**Portfolio View:**
- Capacity indicators: limit, available, consumed, utilization, health status (lines 84-91)
- Color-coded health: green (healthy), yellow (warning), red (critical)
- Live outcome holdings table showing net size, absolute size, last trade price

**System View:**
- Health beacon with glow effect for online status (line 44)
- Platform aggregate stats (volume, liquidity, active count, resolved count)
- Market status breakdown by status
- API surface with route tags

---

### 6. **ROUTING STRUCTURE**

**Framework: React Router v6.28.0** (confirmed in package.json)

**Implementation:**
- **Location:** `/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/app/router.tsx`
- **Pattern:** `createBrowserRouter()` with nested routes
- **Parent Layout:** `AppLayout` (defined in `/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/app/App.tsx`)
- **Navigation:** NavLink components with active state styling (lines 20-22 in App.tsx)

**Data Fetching:**
- **Library:** TanStack React Query v5.60.0 (for API calls and caching)
- **Hooks Location:** `/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/lib/query/hooks.ts`
- **WebSocket Support:** `useMarketPriceSubscription` hook (line 132 of hooks.ts) for real-time price updates

**Session Management:**
- **Location:** `/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/features/session/context.tsx`
- Stores accountId and agentId in context
- Header inputs for account configuration

---

### **TECH STACK SUMMARY**

| Category | Technology |
|----------|------------|
| **Framework** | React 18.3.1 |
| **Routing** | React Router DOM 6.28.0 |
| **State/Data** | TanStack React Query 5.60.0 |
| **Build Tool** | Vite 6.0.0 |
| **Language** | TypeScript 5.6 |
| **Testing** | Vitest, Testing Library |
| **Styling** | CSS Variables + Inline Styles (NO Tailwind/CSS Modules) |
| **Animations** | CSS Transitions + Inline Keyframes (NO Framer Motion/React Spring) |

---

### **KEY FILE LOCATIONS (Absolute Paths)**

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/components/ui/ProbabilityBar.tsx` | Main probability/price display component |
| `/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/components/ui/Spinner.tsx` | Loading animation with keyframes |
| `/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/app/router.tsx` | Route definitions |
| `/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/app/App.tsx` | Main layout and navigation |
| `/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/routes/MarketList.tsx` | Market listing view |
| `/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/routes/MarketDetail.tsx` | Market detail view with probabilities |
| `/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/routes/Portfolio.tsx` | User portfolio view |
| `/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/routes/System.tsx` | System status view |
| `/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/styles/globals.css` | CSS variables and global styles |
| `/tmp/temporal-worktrees/task-bayes-websocket-001/frontend/src/lib/api/types.ts` | TypeScript type definitions |

"""Minimal Bayes Market backend restoring the documented HTTP surface."""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import hashlib
import html
import importlib.util
import json
import math
import os
import re

import threading
import time
from collections import deque
from copy import deepcopy
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from http.server import BaseHTTPRequestHandler, HTTPServer
from pathlib import Path, PurePosixPath
from typing import Any, NamedTuple
from urllib.parse import parse_qs, quote, urlparse

BACKEND_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
PROJECT_ROOT_PATH = BACKEND_DIR.parent
PROJECT_ROOT = str(PROJECT_ROOT_PATH)
if PROJECT_ROOT not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, PROJECT_ROOT)

from backend.inference import (
    COMPILE_RESULT_CACHE,
    CURRENT_MODEL_COMPILER,
    CURRENT_MODEL_QUERY_BACKEND,

    DEFAULT_ENGINE_CONFIG,
    EngineConfig,
    InferenceCompileError,

    canonical_json_hash,
)

FORMULA_SCHEMA_MODULE_PATH = Path(__file__).with_name("formula_schema.py")
_FORMULA_SCHEMA_SPEC = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(
    "bayes_market_formula_schema",
    FORMULA_SCHEMA_MODULE_PATH,
)
if _FORMULA_SCHEMA_SPEC is None or _FORMULA_SCHEMA_SPEC.loader is None:
    raise RuntimeError(f"Unable to load formula schema module from {FORMULA_SCHEMA_MODULE_PATH}")

def emit_terminal_event(command: dict[str, Any], event_type: str, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Append a terminal event to the per-market event journal."""
    market_id = str(command["marketId"])
    with get_market_write_lock(market_id):
        seq = MARKET_EVENT_SEQUENCES.get(market_id, 0) + 1
        prev_event_hash = LAST_EVENT_HASHES.get(market_id, GENESIS_EVENT_HASH)
        event = {
            "schemaVersion": "bayes-event/v1",
            "eventId": generate_event_id(),
            "marketId": market_id,
            "seq": seq,
            "commandId": str(command["commandId"]),
            "eventType": event_type,
            "emittedAt": utc_timestamp(),
            "approxFlag": False,
            "payload": deepcopy(payload),
            "prevEventHash": prev_event_hash,
        }
        event["eventHash"] = canonical_json_hash(event)
        MARKET_EVENT_SEQUENCES[market_id] = seq
        LAST_EVENT_HASHES[market_id] = str(event["eventHash"])
        with _EVENTS_LOCK:
            EVENTS[str(event["eventId"])] = deepcopy(event)
        return event


def build_terminal_result(event: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Build the terminal result block returned to API callers."""
    result = {
        "terminal": True,
        "status": "accepted" if event["eventType"] == "CommandAccepted" else "rejected",
        "eventType": str(event["eventType"]),
        "eventId": str(event["eventId"]),
        "commandId": str(event["commandId"]),
        "emittedAt": str(event["emittedAt"]),

            payload, status = route_request(method, self.path, body, headers=self.headers)
            if status < 400:
                extra_headers = rate_limit_headers(agent_id)
        except ApiError as exc:
            payload, status = error_payload(exc.code, exc.message, **exc.details), exc.status
            if exc.code == "rate_limit_exceeded":
                retry_after = str(exc.details.get("retryAfterSeconds", 1))
                extra_headers = {"Retry-After": retry_after}
                extra_headers.update(rate_limit_headers(str(exc.details.get("agentId", ""))))
        self.send_json(payload, status, extra_headers=extra_headers)

    def _serve_public_text_asset(self, url_path: str) -> bool:
        """Serve a small set of explicit text assets from the project root."""
        path = normalize_path(urlparse(url_path).path)
        if path != HELLO_TEXT_ROUTE or not HELLO_TEXT_PATH.is_file():
            return False

        content = HELLO_TEXT_PATH.read_bytes()
        self.send_response(200)
        self.send_header("Content-Type", "text/plain; charset=utf-8")
        self.send_header("Content-Length", str(len(content)))
        self.send_header("Cache-Control", "no-store")
        self.end_headers()
        self.wfile.write(content)
        return True

    def _serve_static(self, url_path: str) -> bool:
        """Try to serve a static file from frontend/dist/. Return True if served."""
        if self._serve_public_text_asset(url_path):
            return True

        if not FRONTEND_DIST.is_dir():
            return False

        requested_path = normalize_frontend_page_path(url_path)
        clean = requested_path
        if clean == "/":
            clean = "/index.html"

        dist_root = FRONTEND_DIST.resolve()
        candidate = (dist_root / clean.lstrip("/")).resolve()
        try:
            candidate.relative_to(dist_root)
        except ValueError:
            return False

        if not candidate.is_file():
            if not should_fallback_to_frontend_index(clean):
                return False
            candidate = dist_root / "index.html"
            if not candidate.is_file():
                return False

        if candidate == dist_root / "index.html":
            content = render_frontend_index_html(
                candidate.read_text(encoding="utf-8"),
                requested_path,
                headers=self.headers,
            )
        else:
            content = candidate.read_bytes()
        ext = candidate.suffix.lower()
        mime = MIME_TYPES.get(ext, "application/octet-stream")
        self.send_response(200)
        self.send_header("Content-Type", mime)
        self.send_header("Content-Length", str(len(content)))
        if candidate == dist_root / "index.html":
            self.send_header("Cache-Control", "no-store")
        elif clean.startswith("/assets/") or ext in {".js", ".css", ".woff2", ".woff", ".ttf", ".png", ".svg", ".ico"}:
            self.send_header("Cache-Control", "public, max-age=31536000, immutable")
        self.end_headers()
        self.wfile.write(content)
        return True

    def do_GET(self) -> None:  # noqa: N802
        """Serve API routes first, fall back to static frontend files."""
        path = normalize_path(urlparse(self.path).path)
        if path.startswith("/v1/") or path in PUBLIC_HEALTH_ROUTES:
            self.handle_api("GET")
            return
        if path == "/" and "application/json" in (self.headers.get("Accept") or ""):
            self.handle_api("GET")
            return
        if not self._serve_static(self.path):
            self.handle_api("GET")

    def do_POST(self) -> None:  # noqa: N802
        """Serve an HTTP POST request."""
        self.handle_api("POST")

    def do_PUT(self) -> None:  # noqa: N802
        """Serve an HTTP PUT request."""
        self.handle_api("PUT")

    def do_DELETE(self) -> None:  # noqa: N802
        """Serve an HTTP DELETE request."""
        self.handle_api("DELETE")


def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
    """Parse CLI arguments for the standalone backend server."""
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Bayes Market backend server")
    parser.add_argument("--host", default=os.environ.get("BAYES_MARKET_HOST", "127.0.0.1"))
    parser.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=int(os.environ.get("BAYES_MARKET_PORT", "3205")))
    return parser.parse_args()


def run_server(host: str = "127.0.0.1", port: int = 3205) -> None:
    """Start the HTTP server and block forever."""
    HTTPServer((host, port), BayesHandler).serve_forever()
